templates.subject: Welcome to [Business] (start date [date])
templates.body:
Hi [Name],

We're glad you're joining us. Your first day is [date] at [time]. Meet at [location].

Bring: [ID / steel-toe / phone / etc.].

Your point of contact: [Name, phone]. Wear [dress code if any].

You'll get a printed day-1 checklist when you arrive. Reply to confirm you got this.

[Owner name]
[Business]

DAY 1 · [Name] · [Role] · [Date]

[ ] Welcome + tour (restrooms, exits, where to eat)
[ ] Safety: emergency, injury, who to call
[ ] Keys / codes / vehicle rules (if applicable)
[ ] Cash / card / refund rules (if applicable)
[ ] Buddy introduced: [Buddy name, phone]
[ ] Shadow: observe full [route / shift / process]
[ ] End of day: 5-min recap: what was clear? what was confusing?
[ ] Owner sign-off

Buddy assignment: [New hire] → [Buddy]

Buddy responsibilities (days 1-7):
- Answer how-to questions within 15 minutes on shift
- Ride along or check in once per day
- Flag owner if new hire is lost or unsafe

Backup if buddy unreachable: [Name, phone]

New hire: ask buddy first, owner second.

WEEK 1 · Route driver

Day 1-2: Shadow full route. Notes on every stop type.
Day 3: Drive with buddy in passenger seat.
Day 4: Lead route, buddy follows.
Day 5: Solo route, buddy on group text.
Day 6-7: Solo + end-of-day 3-question debrief.

Must demonstrate before solo:
[ ] Machine check procedure
[ ] Cash collection / handoff
[ ] What to do when machine is down
[ ] Who to call (tech, owner)

Laminated truck card: emergency + owner numbers.

WEEK 1 · Warehouse / inventory

Day 1: Walk pick path, label system, where overstock lives.
Day 2: Build one load-out with supervisor.
Day 3: Build load-out solo, supervisor spot-check.
Day 4-5: Par sheet updates with driver feedback loop.
Day 7: Accurate pick list without rework on one route.

Must demonstrate:
[ ] Read par sheet
[ ] Mark shortages correctly
[ ] FIFO / date rotation if food

WEEK 1 · Counter / customer-facing

Day 1: Shadow rush + slow period. Read mf-tpl-001 cheat sheet.
Day 2: POS only, no complaints solo.
Day 3: Complaints with supervisor beside you.
Day 4-5: Solo counter, supervisor on call.
Day 7: Handle mild complaint solo per playbook.

Must demonstrate:
[ ] Opening / closing checklist
[ ] Complaint step 1-2 scripts
[ ] When to escalate to owner

Day 7 check-in (15 minutes):

1. What still feels unclear?
2. What mistake are you most afraid of making?
3. Ready for more autonomy next week, or need more shadow?

Owner notes → log row. Adjust week 2 plan before Monday.

Day 30 review (30 minutes):

1. Role still right fit?
2. Top 3 things you do confidently now
3. One process we should fix for the next hire
4. Autonomy: shadow / partial / full route or shift
5. Compensation or hours conversation if applicable (owner decision)

Log outcome. Update role checklist if something broke in week 1.

templates.use_when: Any vending role with keys or cash
templates.body:
Keys & cash rules · [Business]

- Keys stay [on person / in lockbox]. Never duplicate without owner approval.
- Collections logged same day: amount, machine ID, date.
- Shortages reported immediately, not end of week.
- No lending keys to non-employees.

I have read and understand: _______________ Date: _______

templates.use_when: Replacing someone who quit with no handoff
templates.body:
Crash onboarding (replacement hire):

Day 1 only: safety, keys, one route segment or one station.
Skip nice-to-haves for 72 hours.
Owner writes what previous employee never documented.
Day 7 mandatory: what gaps remain?

Use buddy + laminated minimum viable checklist.

Outgoing → incoming handoff (2 hours together):

[ ] Route map / machine list walkthrough
[ ] Odd machines and account quirks
[ ] Where logs live
[ ] Vendor contacts
[ ] One week overlap if possible

Outgoing writes 5 things only they know.
